_risk_level_doc: Medium: changes agent contracts across 4 markdown files; no infrastructure code changed; no security boundary affected. Incorrect spec could cause downstream dev cycles to silently omit provenance checks.

requirement.original: OOS item D — dev self-report provenance. The dev agent currently reports files_modified and files_created based on its own work-tree inspection at end of execution. In cycle dev-20260519-211515, dev claimed 17 modified + 8 created files that were ALL already present in git HEAD from prior session commits (6d28883, 28a1e85, 4d9f9f5, 6537d7d). The fix has four parts: (1) commands/dev.md Step 8 captures baseline_head_sha and baseline_dirty_snapshot, (2) agents/dev.md mandates git-diff derivation for files_modified, (3) agents/dev.md adds observed_preexisting[], (4) agents/qa.md and agents/changelog-analyst.md check provenance.
requirement.clarified: Establish a four-part markdown-only contract change that ensures dev.files_modified/files_created reflect only files actually changed in this cycle (via git diff vs a pre-dispatch baseline SHA), with clear handling for pre-existing dirty files, and provenance enforcement in QA and changelog-analyst.
requirement.what: Add baseline_head_sha + baseline_dirty_snapshot capture to commands/dev.md Step 8; mandate git-diff derivation in agents/dev.md Output Format; add observed_preexisting[] field; add provenance check to agents/qa.md; add anomaly classification to agents/changelog-analyst.md.
requirement.why: Dev agent self-reporting inflated file counts (pre-existing committed files) corrupts QA scope analysis, changelog-analyst enrichment scope, and commit attribution chain.

included: agents/qa.md provenance check (FAIL on inflated claims) — covers files_modified via git diff AND files_created via git ls-files --others --exclude-standard
agents/changelog-analyst.md provenance_anomaly classification (working-tree diff only, no ..HEAD)
excluded: enforcement tooling beyond the four markdown files

success_criteria: commands/dev.md Step 8 dispatch template contains literal strings 'baseline_head_sha' and 'baseline_dirty_snapshot' in the dispatch payload body
agents/dev.md Output Format describes files_modified/files_created as git-diff-derived and includes observed_preexisting[] and baseline_head_sha fields

agents/qa.md contains a provenance check raising critical FAIL with primary_cause: 'dev_implementation' when files_modified paths are absent from git diff and not in baseline_dirty_snapshot (label: dev_provenance_violation); AND when files_created paths are absent from git ls-files --others --exclude-standard and not in baseline_dirty_snapshot (label: files_created_provenance_violation)
agents/changelog-analyst.md Phase 2 classifies such paths as provenance_anomaly using working-tree diff (no ..HEAD) and excludes them from enrichment scope
Both QA and changelog-analyst gracefully skip when baseline_head_sha is absent (unborn repo edge case)
constraints: No hooks, no settings.json, no scripts — markdown only
Changes must be additive (no existing prose removed or weakened)

Diff command form: working-tree diff (git diff --name-only <sha>, NO ..HEAD) for ALL agent stages — dev, QA, and changelog-analyst Phase 2 alike. Phase 2 runs before staging/commit so ..HEAD returns empty set. For files_created (new untracked files), use git ls-files --others --exclude-standard since untracked files do not appear in git diff output.
observed_preexisting[] is informational only; does not block QA or changelog-analyst

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Dev cycle dev-20260519-211515 claimed 17 modified + 8 created files that were all pre-existing in git HEAD; QA and changelog-analyst accepted the list as ground truth.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: agents/dev.md Output Format does not mandate that files_modified/files_created be derived from a git diff vs a pre-dispatch baseline. No baseline SHA is captured at dispatch time. Dev agents perform work-tree inspection ('is this file in expected state?') rather than diffing, so files already in the correct state from prior commits are counted.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — this is a spec omission, not a regression from a specific commit
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: The dev Output Format was designed for the common case where dev actually edits files; the pre-existing-state scenario was not anticipated.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: QA scope analysis, changelog-analyst enrichment, and commit attribution all consume dev.files_modified as ground truth. Inflated lists cause: (a) QA verifying files it shouldn't; (b) changelog-analyst deriving wrong commit type/scope; (c) audit trails showing false attribution.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Identified in cycle dev-20260519-211515; OOS item D in Codex prioritization report 2026-05-xx.
observations: agents/dev.md Output Format (lines 488-494) describes files_modified and files_created as non-null lists with no baseline SHA constraint — inferred from Read of the file
commands/dev.md Step 8 dispatch prompt (lines 647-673) contains no baseline_head_sha or baseline_dirty_snapshot fields — inferred from Read of the file
agents/qa.md Step 5 Regression Testing (lines 396-430) does not include a provenance check against a baseline SHA — inferred from Read of the file
agents/changelog-analyst.md Phase 2 (lines 82-116) treats dev-report files as authoritative for enrichment with no anomaly classification — inferred from Read of the file
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred only — runtime observation of the dev cycle's actual false attribution is the user's report, not independently reproduced here)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: User cited exact cycle ID (dev-20260519-211515) and specific prior-session commits (6d28883, 28a1e85, 4d9f9f5, 6537d7d). BA read all four target files and confirmed the absence of baseline-capture and diff-derivation language.
root_cause_analysis.located_source: agents/dev.md Output Format section (lines 488-494); commands/dev.md Step 8 dispatch template (lines 647-673)

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: not applicable — spec omission, not a regression from a specific commit

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: the orchestrator (commands/dev.md Step 8) captures baseline_head_sha (the HEAD SHA at dispatch time) and baseline_dirty_snapshot (list of untracked/modified files at dispatch) and passes both into the dev dispatch payload
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: commands/dev.md Step 8 dispatch template includes 'baseline_head_sha' and 'baseline_dirty_snapshot' in the dispatch payload body (not only comments)

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: agents/dev.md specifies that dev.files_modified must be computed from git diff --name-only $baseline_head_sha (working-tree vs baseline, pre-commit — not from work-tree inspection)
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: agents/dev.md Output Format mandates split derivation: files_modified from 'git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>'; files_created from 'git ls-files --others --exclude-standard' excluding paths appearing as untracked entries in baseline_dirty_snapshot; schema includes observed_preexisting[], baseline_head_sha, and baseline_dirty_snapshot as top-level dev-report fields

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: agents/qa.md and agents/changelog-analyst.md add a check: if dev.files_modified lists a path not in git diff vs baseline, raise FAIL with primary_cause = 'dev_implementation' (label: dev_provenance_violation); if dev.files_created lists a path not in git ls-files --others --exclude-standard and not in baseline_dirty_snapshot, raise FAIL with primary_cause = 'dev_implementation' (label: files_created_provenance_violation)
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: agents/qa.md contains provenance check raising FAIL with primary_cause: 'dev_implementation' for both files_modified (dev_provenance_violation via git diff) and files_created (files_created_provenance_violation via git ls-files --others --exclude-standard); agents/changelog-analyst.md uses a split provenance filter: files_modified checked via git diff, files_created checked via git ls-files, both excluding baseline_dirty_snapshot entries; anomaly paths excluded from enrichment

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: **Top-level non-null lists** (CRITICAL): `dev.files_modified` and `dev.files_created` MUST be non-null lists at the `dev` root level
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Ensures dev-report is not missing file lists (required by commit.sh)

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: Extract `dev.files_modified[]` and `dev.files_created[]` arrays from the resolved path. These paths are used for commit message enrichment (deriving type/scope/summary). They do NOT add files to the commit that are not already in git status output.
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Clarifies enrichment-only role of dev-report in commit pipeline

setup.reason: non-UI -- agent-prompt edit; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths

evidence.observed: dev claimed 17 modified + 8 created files that were ALL already present in git HEAD from prior session commits (6d28883, 28a1e85, 4d9f9f5, 6537d7d)

measured.value: agents/dev.md Output Format defines files_modified and files_created as non-null lists with no baseline SHA or git-diff derivation constraint; commands/dev.md Step 8 dispatch template has no baseline_head_sha field

expected.value: files_modified derived from git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>; baseline_head_sha captured at dispatch and passed into dev payload; provenance check in QA and changelog-analyst
expected.source: User requirement verbatim + user requirement document docs/dev/user-requirement-dev-20260522-080646.md

evidence.gap: No baseline SHA is captured; no diff-derivation is mandated; no downstream provenance check exists — allowing inflated file lists to flow undetected through the quality chain

all_occurrences: commands/dev.md:647-673 (Step 8 dispatch template — no baseline fields)
agents/dev.md:488-494 (Output Format files_modified/files_created — no diff derivation)

reference_source.description: Four target markdown files read directly in this session
reference_source.url_or_location: agents/dev.md, commands/dev.md, agents/qa.md, agents/changelog-analyst.md (all read in session)

novelty_check.rationale: No prior attempt exists for this specific provenance issue

context.codebase_state: master branch; recent commits include 1a1d058 (Cycle 2 remediation), 50f2ca2 (style-inspector), 1bf636c (close-report ship), 6537d7d (hooks/commit fix), d23bfb6 (redev align)

development_approach.strategy: Four additive text insertions in existing markdown sections. All changes are new prose; no existing prose is removed or weakened. The key technical subtlety is the SPLIT derivation rule: files_modified uses git diff --name-only <sha> (working-tree form, no ..HEAD) at all agent stages; files_created uses git ls-files --others --exclude-standard since untracked files never appear in git diff output. Phase 2 runs before staging/commit so ..HEAD form is forbidden. The baseline_dirty_snapshot exclusion for files_created applies only to ??-prefixed untracked entries in the parsed snapshot — not to modified-tracked entries.

changes.what: Add a 'Pre-dispatch baseline capture' subsection immediately before the Dev dispatch prompt template. This subsection instructs the orchestrator to capture baseline_head_sha (git rev-parse HEAD) and baseline_dirty_snapshot (git status --porcelain) and include both as fields in the dev dispatch payload.
changes.before_description: Step 8 moves directly from test-writer gate evaluation to the dispatch prompt template with no baseline capture
changes.after_description: Step 8 includes a baseline capture block before dispatch; baseline_head_sha and baseline_dirty_snapshot appear as named fields in the dispatch payload
changes.rationale: Without the baseline capture, dev has no reference point to compute which files it actually changed vs which were already correct

changes.what: Replace the current description of files_modified/files_created ('non-null lists at the dev root level') with a split derivation rule: files_modified from 'git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>' (working tree vs baseline); files_created from 'git ls-files --others --exclude-standard' excluding paths that appeared as untracked entries (??-prefixed) in baseline_dirty_snapshot. Add baseline_head_sha and baseline_dirty_snapshot as top-level dev-report fields. Add observed_preexisting[] field with schema and description.
changes.before_description: files_modified and files_created are non-null lists; no derivation method is specified
changes.after_description: files_modified derived from git diff; files_created derived from git ls-files excluding pre-existing untracked paths; observed_preexisting[] is a separate list for files confirmed correct but absent from both derivation commands; baseline_head_sha and baseline_dirty_snapshot included as top-level fields
changes.rationale: Dev must report only what it actually changed; pre-existing-state confirmation is a separate informational category

changes.section: Step 5 Regression Testing (or new Step 5.5 Provenance Check)
changes.what: Add a provenance check subsection with two sub-checks: (a) for files_modified — read baseline_head_sha from context JSON or dev-report; compute git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha> (working tree vs baseline); for every path in dev.files_modified absent from this diff AND absent from baseline_dirty_snapshot, raise a critical FAIL finding with primary_cause: 'dev_implementation' and label 'dev_provenance_violation'; (b) for files_created — compute git ls-files --others --exclude-standard; for every path in dev.files_created absent from this untracked list AND absent from baseline_dirty_snapshot, raise a critical FAIL finding with primary_cause: 'dev_implementation' and label 'files_created_provenance_violation'. Include graceful skip when baseline_head_sha is absent.
changes.before_description: Step 5 performs git diff HEAD~1 and dependency checks but no provenance check against baseline
changes.after_description: Step 5 includes provenance check that validates dev.files_modified against actual git diff from baseline_head_sha
changes.rationale: QA is the last line of defense before changelog-analyst and commit chain consume the file list

changes.what: After extracting files_modified and files_created from dev-report, add a split provenance filter (Phase 2 runs before staging and commit; the ..HEAD form is FORBIDDEN here — returns empty set pre-commit): (a) for paths in files_modified — compute 'git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>' (working-tree form); paths absent from this diff AND absent from baseline_dirty_snapshot are classified as provenance_anomaly; (b) for paths in files_created — compute 'git ls-files --others --exclude-standard'; paths absent from this untracked list AND absent as ??-prefixed entries in parsed baseline_dirty_snapshot are classified as provenance_anomaly. Exclude provenance_anomaly paths from type/scope/summary enrichment derivation (stage them if in git status, but don't use them for commit message construction).
changes.before_description: Phase 2 uses dev-report files_modified/files_created as authoritative enrichment source; applies single git diff filter to both sets
changes.after_description: Phase 2 applies split provenance filter: git diff for files_modified, git ls-files for files_created; anomaly paths are still staged but excluded from commit message scope derivation
changes.rationale: Commit message attribution must reflect actual changes, not pre-existing files dev found in correct state

development_approach.validation_approach: QA verifies by reading the four modified files and confirming: (1) baseline_head_sha and baseline_dirty_snapshot appear in commands/dev.md Step 8 dispatch payload; (2) agents/dev.md mandates git-diff derivation and has observed_preexisting[] and baseline_head_sha fields; (3) agents/qa.md provenance check exists with correct FAIL semantics; (4) agents/changelog-analyst.md has provenance_anomaly classification and enrichment exclusion rule.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex exec (gpt-5.5 xhigh) ran on iteration 3 draft. 5 findings: (1) baseline_dirty_snapshot captured with --porcelain may aggregate untracked dirs as '?? dir/' vs file-level ls-files — fix with -uall flag and parse only ??-prefixed lines for files_created exclusion; (2) confirmed the files_created baseline exclusion is semantically correct but needs precise wording specifying '??-prefixed entries'; (3) AC2 must include ls-files pattern to verify agents/dev.md was updated for split derivation — AC6 only covers QA; (4) AC4 must split filter in THEN clause and check.patterns_present to include ls-files for changelog-analyst; (5) AC4 ..HEAD absent check should target executable command form not explanatory text (minor, was already F3 from iter2 QA).

findings.issue: baseline_dirty_snapshot captured with --porcelain may aggregate untracked dirs as '?? dir/' while ls-files enumerates '?? dir/file' — exclusion comparison can fail at directory boundary
findings.proposed_fix: Parse only ??-prefixed lines from baseline_dirty_snapshot for files_created exclusion. The fix is in M2/M5 wording (now says 'paths that appeared as untracked entries (??-prefixed lines) in the parsed baseline_dirty_snapshot') and the development_approach instruction for agents/dev.md.
findings.rationale: Codex finding #1 is correct — the parsing semantics must be explicit to avoid directory-vs-file mismatch at exclusion time. Applied by adding ??-prefixed qualifier to M2, M5, and AC4 THEN wording.

findings.issue: Wording 'paths in baseline_dirty_snapshot' is ambiguous — snapshot includes modified-tracked files, not just untracked ones
findings.proposed_fix: Updated M2 and M5 to say 'paths that appeared as untracked entries (??-prefixed lines) in the parsed baseline_dirty_snapshot'. AC4 THEN updated accordingly.
findings.rationale: Precision is required: only ?? entries in the snapshot are pre-existing untracked files. Modified-tracked entries (M, A, D prefixes) are irrelevant to files_created exclusion.

findings.issue: AC2 check.patterns did not include 'ls-files --others --exclude-standard', so QA grep of agents/dev.md would not verify the split derivation rule for files_created was actually written
findings.proposed_fix: AC2 check.patterns updated from 3 to 4 items, adding 'ls-files --others --exclude-standard'. AC2 THEN clause updated to explicitly state both git diff (files_modified) and git ls-files (files_created) derivation rules.
findings.rationale: This is the core F1 fix for AC2. Without adding ls-files to AC2.check.patterns, QA cannot verify that agents/dev.md was correctly updated to the split derivation rule.

findings.issue: AC4 THEN clause and check.patterns still described only git diff for files_created in changelog-analyst — split M5 filter not reflected in AC4
findings.proposed_fix: AC4 THEN updated to specify split filter: files_modified via git diff, files_created via git ls-files. AC4 check.patterns_present now includes 'ls-files --others --exclude-standard'. AC4 check field renamed from 'pattern_absent_in_provenance_filter_step_1' to 'pattern_absent_as_actual_diff_command' with command-scoped regex.
findings.rationale: This is the core F2 fix for AC4. Without updating AC4, QA would verify changelog-analyst spec without checking that the ls-files split is present for files_created.

findings.issue: AC4 ..HEAD absent check uses regex that could match explanatory refutation text ('..HEAD form is WRONG') not just executable commands
findings.proposed_fix: AC4 check field renamed 'pattern_absent_as_actual_diff_command' with pattern 'diff\s+--name-only\s+\S*\.\.\S*HEAD' — matches only when ..HEAD appears as an actual git diff argument, not in explanatory text.
findings.rationale: Was F3 (non-blocking) in iter2 QA report. Applied in iter3 since it's a precision fix that prevents false-positive failures in the AC4 grep check.

findings.issue: Staged-but-not-committed new files are not covered by git ls-files --others --exclude-standard
findings.rationale: Dev agents do not stage before reporting. This edge case is pre-existing scope-out from iter2. Out of scope for this correction.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Iteration 3 corrections applied: (1) M2 split derivation rule — files_modified via git diff, files_created via git ls-files excluding ??-prefixed untracked entries from baseline_dirty_snapshot; (2) M5 split provenance filter — same split for changelog-analyst Phase 2, with ??-prefixed qualifier on exclusion; (3) AC2 THEN clause and check.patterns updated to require ls-files pattern in agents/dev.md; (4) AC4 THEN clause updated to require split filter, check.patterns_present extended to include ls-files, check field renamed with command-scoped regex; (5) Technical Hints updated to explain split derivation and ??-prefix parsing semantics; (6) context JSON R2/R3 acceptance_criterion, development_approach, and strategy updated to reflect split.
